label_verbatim: (Name of Maine or Foreign Limited Liability Company)
rationale: Top-of-form LLC name. Form serves both domestic and foreign LLCs from a single 'entity.name' widget — no separate entity.home_jurisdiction_name slot is provided here, unlike the Application for Authority filings.

label_verbatim: FIRST: On ___ (filing date) the Secretary of State filed a document delivered for filing by the undersigned limited liability company entitled
rationale: Date the original document being corrected was filed with the SOS.

label_verbatim: limited liability company entitled: ___ (i.e. Application for Authority to do Business, Assumed Name, etc.)
rationale: Title of the original document being corrected (e.g., 'Application for Authority to do Business', 'Statement of Assumed Name').

label_verbatim: THIRD: The incorrect or inaccurate information to be corrected and the reason it is incorrect or inaccurate or the manner in which the signing was defective is described as follows
rationale: Large multiline text widget for describing the error and the reason for correction.

label_verbatim: FOURTH: The correction of the incorrect or inaccurate information or the correction to the manner in which the signing was defective is described as follows
rationale: Large multiline text widget for describing the corrected information.

label_verbatim: DATED ___
rationale: Date the Statement of Correction is signed. Single Dated field shared across both signer slots.

label_verbatim: *Authorized person (block 1): (signature) / (type or print name and capacity)
rationale: First authorized signer's combined name+capacity widget. Shape D (matches MBCA-11 / MLLC-12 / MBCA-21 / MBCA-20). The form provides two parallel signature blocks ('*Authorized person' x2); the first is treated as the primary signer.

label_verbatim: *Authorized person (block 2): (signature) / (type or print name and capacity)
rationale: Second authorized signer's combined name+capacity widget (Shape D, optional). Used when more than one authorized person co-signs the correction. Per-signer numbering (signer_2) parallels the merger-form pattern (merger.parties[N].signature_block.signer_1/signer_2 on MBCA-10) but at the filing level rather than per-party.
